lab_notes: Official website found for LCOE: https://www.lcoe.es. LCOE (Laboratorio Central Oficial de Electrotecnia) is operated by FUNDACIÓN PARA EL FOMENTO DE LA INNOVACIÓN INDUSTRIAL (F2I2; CIF G80455231, Madrid). F2I2 frames LCOE as institutionally continuous since 1949 (creation of the original electrotechnical lab), with the current foundation structure established in 1992 after reconversion of the ETSII/UPM industrial labs (“Patronato de Laboratorios Industriales”). LCOE also sits in international conformity-testing infrastructure as an IECEE CB Testing Laboratory (CBTL) under AENOR responsibility (certificate TL083; acceptance documentation issued 2018, with IECEE acceptance history shown in later updates). In Spain’s ministerial designation landscape, LCOE appears in MINTUR’s Jun 2025 roster as ST0001 (designated “Servicio Técnico” in the EU vehicle/type-approval ecosystem), with F2I2’s UCA separately listed as ST0113 (certification/CoP-type functions).
eu_DoC: EU Declaration of Conformity (manufacturer DoC, updated version). DoC holder/responsible manufacturer: KEPAR ELECTRONICA S.L. (Spain; Zaragoza). Product identified as 'FlashLed SOS' (trademark: FLASHLED; V16 emergency flashlight with GNSS + NB-IoT; 8×LED; 9 Vdc). Declares conformity with RED 2014/53/EU and RoHS (2011/65/EU as amended by (EU) 2015/863) and lists harmonised standards for safety/EMC/radio (GNSS + NB-IoT). Additionally addresses RED Article 3(3)(d/e/f) cybersecurity requirements, referencing EN 18031-1:2025 and EN 18031-2:2025 (note: the OJEU-harmonised references are EN 18031-1:2024 and EN 18031-2:2024; the ':2025' dates correspond to the UNE-EN Spanish national adoption). The DoC header claims scope 'd, e, f' but EN 18031-3 (financial transactions, Art. 3.3(f)) is not listed; Art. 3.3(f) is not expected to apply to a V-16 emergency flashlight. Also declares compliance with EU Battery Regulation 2023/1542 (Annex III). No Notified Body or EU-Type Examination Certificate is referenced; EN 18031-1/-2 were harmonised with restrictions (re: password bypass in clauses 6.2.5.1/6.2.5.2), and self-assessment is only valid if those restrictions do not apply to the product. No conformity assessment module (Annex II/III/IV) is stated. A prior-version EU Declaration of Conformity was also located during collection (source: indexed search-engine result; hosted at media.ircongas.com). That DoC identifies product as 'Flashled SOS' and declares conformity with RED 2014/53/EU and RoHS but does not address RED Article 3(3)(d/e/f) cybersecurity requirements, does not reference EN 18031-x, and does not cover battery regulatory compliance under EU Regulation 2023/1542. Source: provided by Turisport S.L. via email on 4 March 2026 in response to a documentation request sent on 3 March 2026. Delivered as LCOE.zip attachment (4 MB) containing three brand-specific DoCs.

brand_notes: Brand, solicitante, and fabricante all share a single website for ERUM Group: https://erumgroup.com/. The Group commercialises clothes hangers and V16s. The Group's device profile leads to a specific LedOne website: https://ledone.eco/es/. Official website found for LEDONE: https://ledone.eco/es/ (consumer-facing ecommerce site associated with ERUM GROUP (mother company); direct purchase flow observed). The site markets “LEDoNE Connected” as a DGT-homologated, connected/geolocated V16 beacon; it also sells two V16 product variants: “Baliza v16 LEDONE Connected” (https://ledone.eco/es/product/baliza-v16-led-one), featuring now certificate number, connectivity info, or relevant documentation except for a general downloadable marketing and usability brochure (https://ledone.eco/wp-content/uploads/2025/11/Instrucciones-de-la-Baliza-obligatoria-LEDONE-1.pdf); and “Baliza V16 ASEICA” (https://ledone.eco/es/product/baliza-aseica – includes 10-euro donation to cancer research association ASEICA), featuring no certificate number, connectivity info, or relevant documentation. The site footer displays two LCOE certificate badge numbers: LCOE 2025020090G1 and LCOE 2023010084G1 linked to the respective DGT certificate PDFs (https://ledone.eco/wp-content/uploads/2025/11/LCOE-2025020090G1.pdf, https://ledone.eco/wp-content/uploads/2026/01/LCOE-2023010084G1_Ext-1-2025070646G1-3.pdf – Certificate number on DGT whitelist and LEDOne badge (LCOE 2023010084G1) does not match certificate number on PDF document available for download on DGT or LEDOne sites (LCOE 2025070646G1)). A dedicated “Certificaciones” page lists downloadable/visible documentation beyond the LCOE certificates, including UNE-EN 15343 plus management-system certificates labelled ISO 14001, ISO 14006, and ISO 27001, and the site publishes multiple governance/policy links (legal notice, web/app privacy, cookies, information security policy, human rights policy).

lab_notes: IDIADA (Applus+ IDIADA) is a major automotive design/engineering/testing & homologation lab complex in Santa Oliva (Tarragona) and a high-throughput laboratory node in Spain’s V16 certification ecosystem. The operator is Applus (Applus Services, S.A.), which describes IDIADA Automotive Technology, S.A. as run under an exclusive concession since 1999 with an 80/20 ownership split (Applus+/Government of Catalonia). The 2024 reconcession was structured as a reversion/tender cycle: CNMV disclosures state Applus’s 80% stake was subject to reversion at contract end (Sept 2024) and the Generalitat tendered a new 25-year term; public reporting confirms €428M paid for the 80% block with Generalitat retaining 20%. Generalitat “Idiada 2” planning positions the site to expand into connected/autonomous vehicle testing and homologation, raising independence/market-structure relevance for connected-device regimes.

Official corporate site found for Xanlite (https://www.xanlite.com), a French lighting company, but a search on the company site and broader web for V16 devices yielded no relevant results. Not verified as an official product site for the certified V16. Official Nicar website located (jucarled.com; shares address and infrastructure with jucarauto.com in Zhongshan, China). Site includes a generic “V16 Warning Light with GPS Function” product page but does not reference DGT homologation or LCOE certificate number. Product images show certificate number LCOE 2023080717G1. Branding variations observed (with and without Alert Wise marking). Official Aleu corporate domain located (aleu.com; under construction). Features a single V16 device with IDIADA certificate number (visible on packaging: PC25060037). No evidence of multiple product lines. Company EuroLight Signal S.L. registered with CIF B21619630 on 9/5/2025. Brand-web presence consistent but minimal. Official All Ride website exists (allride.es) matching the logo shown in the DGT certificate. Brand corresponds to a physical bicycle shop in Madrid (Barajas). Website catalog exclusively features bicycles and cycling accessories. No V16 device listed on site. Google searches for “All Ride baliza” or “All Ride V16” return no relevant product results. No observable commercial link between the brand’s public activity and a V16 emergency beacon product.

lab_notes: Official website found for LCOE: https://www.lcoe.es. LCOE (Laboratorio Central Oficial de Electrotecnia) is operated by FUNDACIÓN PARA EL FOMENTO DE LA INNOVACIÓN INDUSTRIAL (F2I2; CIF G80455231, Madrid). F2I2 frames LCOE as institutionally continuous since 1949 (creation of the original electrotechnical lab), with the current foundation structure established in 1992 after reconversion of the ETSII/UPM industrial labs (“Patronato de Laboratorios Industriales”). LCOE also sits in international conformity-testing infrastructure as an IECEE CB Testing Laboratory (CBTL) under AENOR responsibility (certificate TL083; acceptance documentation issued 2018, with IECEE acceptance history shown in later updates). In Spain’s ministerial designation landscape, LCOE appears in MINTUR’s Jun 2025 roster as ST0001 (designated “Servicio Técnico” in the EU vehicle/type-approval ecosystem), with F2I2’s UCA separately listed as ST0113 (certification/CoP-type functions).
eu_DoC: EU Declaration of Conformity (manufacturer DoC, brand-specific). DoC holder/responsible manufacturer: KEPAR ELECTRONICA S.L. (Spain; Zaragoza). Product identified as 'FlashLed SOS V16' (trademark: RACC Mobililty Club [sic — typographical error in DoC: 'Mobililty' instead of 'Mobility']; V16 emergency flashlight with GNSS + NB-IoT; 8×LED; 9 Vdc). Declares conformity with RED 2014/53/EU and RoHS (2011/65/EU as amended by (EU) 2015/863) and lists harmonised standards for safety/EMC/radio (GNSS + NB-IoT). Additionally addresses RED Article 3(3)(d/e/f) cybersecurity requirements, referencing EN 18031-1:2025 and EN 18031-2:2025 (note: the OJEU-harmonised references are EN 18031-1:2024 and EN 18031-2:2024; the ':2025' dates correspond to the UNE-EN Spanish national adoption). The DoC header claims scope 'd, e, f' but EN 18031-3 (financial transactions, Art. 3.3(f)) is not listed; Art. 3.3(f) is not expected to apply to a V-16 emergency flashlight. Also declares compliance with EU Battery Regulation 2023/1542 (Annex III). No Notified Body or EU-Type Examination Certificate is referenced; EN 18031-1/-2 were harmonised with restrictions (re: password bypass in clauses 6.2.5.1/6.2.5.2), and self-assessment is only valid if those restrictions do not apply to the product. No conformity assessment module (Annex II/III/IV) is stated. A prior-version generic EU Declaration of Conformity (not brand-specific to RACC Mobility Club) was also located during collection (source: indexed search-engine result; hosted at media.ircongas.com). That DoC identifies product as 'Flashled SOS' and declares conformity with RED 2014/53/EU and RoHS but does not address RED Article 3(3)(d/e/f) cybersecurity requirements, does not reference EN 18031-x, and does not cover battery regulatory compliance under EU Regulation 2023/1542. Source: provided by Turisport S.L. via email on 4 March 2026 in response to a documentation request sent on 3 March 2026. Delivered as LCOE.zip attachment (4 MB) containing three brand-specific DoCs.

eu_DoC: EU Declaration of Conformity (manufacturer DoC) — two versions collected. DoC holder/responsible manufacturer: OVERSUNENERGY, S.L. (Spain; Paterna, Valencia). Product identified as 'Baliza Conectada,' model T912 (V16 emergency beacon with NB-IoT + GNSS). Original DoC (15/05/2024): listed radio interfaces (Wi-Fi, Bluetooth, LTE) that the updated DoC (16/10/2025) states are not present in the commercialised product; declares conformity with RED 2014/53/EU, RoHS, WEEE, and EMC Directive; lists harmonised standards for safety/EMC/radio including EN 62311:2008 (a later version EN 62311:2020 has since been published) and EN 60950-1 (withdrawn and replaced by EN 62368-1, mandatory from 20 December 2020); no EN 18031 cybersecurity references. Updated DoC + Annex (16/10/2025): corrects product to NB-IoT (3GPP Rel. 13/14) and GNSS only; argues EN 18031 and RED Art. 3(3)(d/e/f) do not apply on the basis that the T912 is not 'internet-connected radio equipment,' citing closed private-APN architecture with no internet breakout, absence of Wi-Fi/BLE/LTE radios, and immutable connectivity configuration; cites CTI RoHS report (A2250174576103, PASS) and CCIC WEEE report (20240210R03118X-2, 92.22% reuse/recycling). Scope not addressed in this DoC: EN 18031 exemption in the updated DoC is based on a private-APN 'not internet-connected' argument; this project did not assess how that argument would be evaluated by market surveillance authorities. battery regulatory compliance (EU) 2023/1542 not set out in either version. lab_activity: CNAE: 7490 Otras actividades profesionales, científicas y técnicas n.c.o.p.
lab_notes: Official website found for LCOE: https://www.lcoe.es. LCOE (Laboratorio Central Oficial de Electrotecnia) is operated by FUNDACIÓN PARA EL FOMENTO DE LA INNOVACIÓN INDUSTRIAL (F2I2; CIF G80455231, Madrid). F2I2 frames LCOE as institutionally continuous since 1949 (creation of the original electrotechnical lab), with the current foundation structure established in 1992 after reconversion of the ETSII/UPM industrial labs (“Patronato de Laboratorios Industriales”). LCOE also sits in international conformity-testing infrastructure as an IECEE CB Testing Laboratory (CBTL) under AENOR responsibility (certificate TL083; acceptance documentation issued 2018, with IECEE acceptance history shown in later updates). In Spain’s ministerial designation landscape, LCOE appears in MINTUR’s Jun 2025 roster as ST0001 (designated “Servicio Técnico” in the EU vehicle/type-approval ecosystem), with F2I2’s UCA separately listed as ST0113 (certification/CoP-type functions).
eu_DoC: EU Declaration of Conformity (manufacturer DoC, updated version). DoC holder/responsible manufacturer: KEPAR ELECTRONICA S.L. (Spain; Zaragoza). Product identified as 'FlashLed SOS V2' (trademark: FLASHLED; V16 emergency flashlight with GNSS + NB-IoT; 8×LED; 9 Vdc). Declares conformity with RED 2014/53/EU and RoHS (2011/65/EU as amended by (EU) 2015/863) and lists harmonised standards for safety/EMC/radio (GNSS + NB-IoT). Additionally addresses RED Article 3(3)(d/e/f) cybersecurity requirements, referencing EN 18031-1:2025 and EN 18031-2:2025 (note: the OJEU-harmonised references are EN 18031-1:2024 and EN 18031-2:2024; the ':2025' dates correspond to the UNE-EN Spanish national adoption). The DoC header claims scope 'd, e, f' but EN 18031-3 (financial transactions, Art. 3.3(f)) is not listed; Art. 3.3(f) is not expected to apply to a V-16 emergency flashlight. Also declares compliance with EU Battery Regulation 2023/1542 (Annex III). No Notified Body or EU-Type Examination Certificate is referenced; EN 18031-1/-2 were harmonised with restrictions (re: password bypass in clauses 6.2.5.1/6.2.5.2), and self-assessment is only valid if those restrictions do not apply to the product. No conformity assessment module (Annex II/III/IV) is stated. A prior-version EU Declaration of Conformity was also located during collection (source: indexed search-engine result; hosted at media.ircongas.com). That DoC identifies product as 'Flashled SOS' (not the 'V2' variant designation) and declares conformity with RED 2014/53/EU and RoHS but does not address RED Article 3(3)(d/e/f) cybersecurity requirements, does not reference EN 18031-x, and does not cover battery regulatory compliance under EU Regulation 2023/1542. In Spain’s ministerial designation landscape, LCOE appears in MINTUR’s Jun 2025 roster as ST0001 (designated “Servicio Técnico” in the EU vehicle/type-approval ecosystem), with F2I2’s UCA separately listed as ST0113 (certification/CoP-type functions).

No product catalogue, datasheets, manuals, DoCs, certificates, or purchase/distribution channels are provided on limburg-tech.com itself. Official website found for Challux: https://www.challux.com. lab_notes: Official website found for LCOE: https://www.lcoe.es. LCOE (Laboratorio Central Oficial de Electrotecnia) is operated by FUNDACIÓN PARA EL FOMENTO DE LA INNOVACIÓN INDUSTRIAL (F2I2; CIF G80455231, Madrid). F2I2 frames LCOE as institutionally continuous since 1949 (creation of the original electrotechnical lab), with the current foundation structure established in 1992 after reconversion of the ETSII/UPM industrial labs (“Patronato de Laboratorios Industriales”). LCOE also sits in international conformity-testing infrastructure as an IECEE CB Testing Laboratory (CBTL) under AENOR responsibility (certificate TL083; acceptance documentation issued 2018, with IECEE acceptance history shown in later updates). In Spain’s ministerial designation landscape, LCOE appears in MINTUR’s Jun 2025 roster as ST0001 (designated “Servicio Técnico” in the EU vehicle/type-approval ecosystem), with F2I2’s UCA separately listed as ST0113 (certification/CoP-type functions).

lab_notes: IDIADA (Applus+ IDIADA) is a major automotive design/engineering/testing & homologation lab complex in Santa Oliva (Tarragona) and a high-throughput laboratory node in Spain’s V16 certification ecosystem. The operator is Applus (Applus Services, S.A.), which describes IDIADA Automotive Technology, S.A. as run under an exclusive concession since 1999 with an 80/20 ownership split (Applus+/Government of Catalonia). The 2024 reconcession was structured as a reversion/tender cycle: CNMV disclosures state Applus’s 80% stake was subject to reversion at contract end (Sept 2024) and the Generalitat tendered a new 25-year term; public reporting confirms €428M paid for the 80% block with Generalitat retaining 20%. Generalitat “Idiada 2” planning positions the site to expand into connected/autonomous vehicle testing and homologation, raising independence/market-structure relevance for connected-device regimes.

solicitante_notes: No official website found for LEDEL SOLUTIONS CO., LTD. LEDEL SOLUTIONS CO., LTD (UK; Co. No. 14734987) holds 33 of the 294 certificates in the corpus (≈11.2%), all issued by IDIADA, all with the same manufacturer: YUYAO JIMING ELECTRONIC CO., LTD (China) with the same underlying platform reused across ~30 trade marks/brands and only a few models (mainly “V16IoT”, plus some “V16IoT/2”). Companies House shows LEDEL incorporated 16/03/2023 at 41 Devonshire Street, Ground Floor Office 1, London W1G 7AJ (a multi-tenant registered-office service address, with 2,200+ companies currently registered at the address per Companies House) and filing dormant accounts for YE 31/03/2024 and 31/03/2025 (£80,000 called-up share capital not paid). A long-form homologation dossier for IDIADA PC24030069 (DAEWOO / V16IoT) ties the platform to specific HW/SW identifiers and documents NB-IoT/“DGT 3.0” connectivity testing and Telefónica onboarding; it also includes a product-family declaration indicating the same hardware is reused across applicants with only firmware digits varying. Dossier maps RED evidence to safety/EMC/radio (3.1(a), 3.1(b), 3.2) but does not include documentation of the RED cybersecurity essential requirements (3(3)(d/e/f)) applicable from 01/08/2025.

lab_notes: IDIADA (Applus+ IDIADA) is a major automotive design/engineering/testing & homologation lab complex in Santa Oliva (Tarragona) and a high-throughput laboratory node in Spain’s V16 certification ecosystem. The operator is Applus (Applus Services, S.A.), which describes IDIADA Automotive Technology, S.A. as run under an exclusive concession since 1999 with an 80/20 ownership split (Applus+/Government of Catalonia). The 2024 reconcession was structured as a reversion/tender cycle: CNMV disclosures state Applus’s 80% stake was subject to reversion at contract end (Sept 2024) and the Generalitat tendered a new 25-year term; public reporting confirms €428M paid for the 80% block with Generalitat retaining 20%. Generalitat “Idiada 2” planning positions the site to expand into connected/autonomous vehicle testing and homologation, raising independence/market-structure relevance for connected-device regimes.

solicitante_notes: Official website found for Speed Sound, S.A. (Spain): https://speedsound.es/. The site is primarily a general corporate/product catalogue (e.g., EV charging products and other automotive-related categories). The V16 landing page (“Seguridad al volante”) lists two V16 devices: “Car Guard Light” and “Claim Light NB IoT”. The V16 landing page contains marketing copy and does not include a DGT homologation certificate number, Declaration of Conformity reference, or links to user manuals, technical sheets, or device identifiers. “Claim Light NB IoT” is presented as a short bullet list (NB-IoT / Telefónica / DGT 3.0 / coverage to 2038) with no product profile page and no downloadable documentation. “Car Guard Light” has a “Descubre” button linking to a one-page brochure PDF (https://speedsound.es/wp-content/uploads/2024/12/ficha-carguardlight.pdf); the brochure lists basic product specifications (IP54, batteries, 360° visibility) but contains no DGT certificate identifier and no formal DoC language. The site includes a contact form and footer contact details (address in Palau-solità i Plegamans, Barcelona; phone/email); no purchase option, no V16-specific distributor links, and no beacon-specific privacy/data-processing documentation are provided on the site.

lab_notes: Official website found for LCOE: https://www.lcoe.es. LCOE (Laboratorio Central Oficial de Electrotecnia) is operated by FUNDACIÓN PARA EL FOMENTO DE LA INNOVACIÓN INDUSTRIAL (F2I2; CIF G80455231, Madrid). F2I2 frames LCOE as institutionally continuous since 1949 (creation of the original electrotechnical lab), with the current foundation structure established in 1992 after reconversion of the ETSII/UPM industrial labs (“Patronato de Laboratorios Industriales”). LCOE also sits in international conformity-testing infrastructure as an IECEE CB Testing Laboratory (CBTL) under AENOR responsibility (certificate TL083; acceptance documentation issued 2018, with IECEE acceptance history shown in later updates). In Spain’s ministerial designation landscape, LCOE appears in MINTUR’s Jun 2025 roster as ST0001 (designated “Servicio Técnico” in the EU vehicle/type-approval ecosystem), with F2I2’s UCA separately listed as ST0113 (certification/CoP-type functions).

solicitante_notes: Official website found for LIMBURG TECHNOLOGY LIMITED: http://www.limburg-tech.com/ (not https). Site is a bare, largely non-interactive single-page landing with generic marketing claims (“Professional Connected V16 Beacon Manufacturer”) and no verifiable corporate disclosures (no legal notice, registered address, company number, VAT/CIF, management/ownership, or product-to-certificate mapping). The page explicitly describes Limburg as a “professional connected V16 beacon manufacturer in China” and as a “related company of Cixi Challux Technology Co.,Ltd.”; the only functional navigation observed is a footer link labelled “Official website” which redirects to challux.com (separately reviewed), a “Contact us” link that opens the local mail client, and “Privacy policy” / “Terms of service” links that loop back to the same landing page. No product catalogue, datasheets, manuals, DoCs, certificates, or purchase/distribution channels are provided on limburg-tech.com itself. Official website found for Challux: https://www.challux.com. Site features physical address: RM 1505,XIANGGE BUILDING,No 1277 DEVELOPMENT ROAD,CIXI CITY,ZHEJIANG PROV,CHINA; pictures of workshop facilities: https://www.challux.com/about-us/; and a products section featuring exclusively ten V16 devices: five featuring certificate number IDIADA PC23100037 and five IDIADA PC21020060. IDIADA PC21020060 does not appear on the current or past DGT homologation lists reviewed during collection. Per Companies House filings for LIMBURG TECHNOLOGY LIMITED: SIC 99999 (Dormant Company); dormant accounts (called-up share capital not paid £20,000; net assets £20,000); four registered-office changes (291 Brighton Rd CR2 6EQ → CH default address Jan–Mar 2025 → 53 Whateleys Dr CV8 2GY → Adamson House M20 2YY → 37 Croydon Rd BR3 4AB); two compulsory strike-off cycles (25/02/2025–19/03/2025; 02/09/2025–28/01/2026), both discontinued following registered-office correction; corporate secretaries of record Yunma Tianlong International Consulting Co., Limited (Co. lab_notes: IDIADA (Applus+ IDIADA) is a major automotive design/engineering/testing & homologation lab complex in Santa Oliva (Tarragona) and a high-throughput laboratory node in Spain’s V16 certification ecosystem. The operator is Applus (Applus Services, S.A.), which describes IDIADA Automotive Technology, S.A. as run under an exclusive concession since 1999 with an 80/20 ownership split (Applus+/Government of Catalonia). The 2024 reconcession was structured as a reversion/tender cycle: CNMV disclosures state Applus’s 80% stake was subject to reversion at contract end (Sept 2024) and the Generalitat tendered a new 25-year term; public reporting confirms €428M paid for the 80% block with Generalitat retaining 20%. Generalitat “Idiada 2” planning positions the site to expand into connected/autonomous vehicle testing and homologation, raising independence/market-structure relevance for connected-device regimes.

No. 14734987) holds 33 of the 294 certificates in the corpus (≈11.2%), all issued by IDIADA, all with the same manufacturer: YUYAO JIMING ELECTRONIC CO., LTD (China) with the same underlying platform reused across ~30 trade marks/brands and only a few models (mainly “V16IoT”, plus some “V16IoT/2”). Companies House shows LEDEL incorporated 16/03/2023 at 41 Devonshire Street, Ground Floor Office 1, London W1G 7AJ (a multi-tenant registered-office service address, with 2,200+ companies currently registered at the address per Companies House) and filing dormant accounts for YE 31/03/2024 and 31/03/2025 (£80,000 called-up share capital not paid). A long-form homologation dossier for IDIADA PC24030069 (DAEWOO / V16IoT) ties the platform to specific HW/SW identifiers and documents NB-IoT/“DGT 3.0” connectivity testing and Telefónica onboarding; it also includes a product-family declaration indicating the same hardware is reused across applicants with only firmware digits varying. Dossier maps RED evidence to safety/EMC/radio (3.1(a), 3.1(b), 3.2) but does not include documentation of the RED cybersecurity essential requirements (3(3)(d/e/f)) applicable from 01/08/2025.

eu_DoC: EC Declarations of Conformity provided by DONGGUAN CHONG GUANG LIGHTING TECHNOLOGY CO. LTD. cover models 'MOD2026 PLUS' and 'MOD100 PLUS.' Both DoCs state IDIADA Automotive Technology S.A. performed specific examination under Real Decreto 1030/2022 (amending RD 159/2021) and issued certificate No. PC25020294 — which is this row's own DGT certificate number. However, the DoCs identify the products as 'MOD2026 PLUS' and 'MOD100 PLUS,' not 'MOD 2026' as designated on this DGT certificate; no exact-match EU Declaration of Conformity for the 'MOD 2026' model designation was found in the sources collected for this project. RED 2014/53/EU and RoHS (2011/65/EU as amended by (EU) 2015/863) applicability is expected for connected radio equipment, but the model-name discrepancy means variant-level conformity cannot be asserted from the documentation available. Companies House shows LEDEL incorporated 16/03/2023 at 41 Devonshire Street, Ground Floor Office 1, London W1G 7AJ (a multi-tenant registered-office service address, with 2,200+ companies currently registered at the address per Companies House) and filing dormant accounts for YE 31/03/2024 and 31/03/2025 (£80,000 called-up share capital not paid). A long-form homologation dossier for IDIADA PC24030069 (DAEWOO / V16IoT) ties the platform to specific HW/SW identifiers and documents NB-IoT/“DGT 3.0” connectivity testing and Telefónica onboarding; it also includes a product-family declaration indicating the same hardware is reused across applicants with only firmware digits varying. Dossier maps RED evidence to safety/EMC/radio (3.1(a), 3.1(b), 3.2) but does not include documentation of the RED cybersecurity essential requirements (3(3)(d/e/f)) applicable from 01/08/2025.

The site strongly markets long-duration connectivity (“conectividad ilimitada incluida” / “13 años” messaging) and “conectado a DGT 3.0” positioning, with product listing/compare pages and direct purchase flow. Site provides an explicit product-to-certificate mapping of the eight different V16 devices on its own product page and purchase flow within site. Device with certificate number LCOE 2025100950G1 (https://geobaliza.com/products/baliza-geobaliza-iot-v16-conectada-y-certificada-dgt) features GEOBALIZA branding, a brief product description, Telefónica connectivity, downloadable data sheet (https://cdn.shopify.com/s/files/1/0918/8786/2086/files/Ficha-Tecnica-GEOBALIZA-IoT-v3.pdf?v=1770888766) and user manual (https://cdn.shopify.com/s/files/1/0918/8786/2086/files/Manual-de-uso-GEOBALIZA-IoT-V16.pdf?v=1765995003). Device with certificate number LCOE 2025020066G1 (https://geobaliza.com/products/baliza-v16-trophy-conectada-certificada-dgt) features TROPHY branding, a brief product description, Telefónica connectivity, downloadable data sheet (https://cdn.shopify.com/s/files/1/0918/8786/2086/files/FT_TROPHY_OK.pdf?v=1766569978) but no user manual. Device with certificate number LCOE 2024070742G1 (https://geobaliza.com/products/baliza-help-flash-iot-conectada-y-certificada-dgt) features Help Flash + branding, a brief product description, Telefónica connectivity, downloadable data sheet (https://cdn.shopify.com/s/files/1/0918/8786/2086/files/Ficha-Tecnica-Baliza-v16-Helpflash-IoT-plus-v2.pdf?v=1770292136) but no user manual. However, product description later refers to certificate number as LCOE 2022110790G1 and data sheet corresponds to LCOE 2025070664G1. Device with certificate number IDIADA PC25040020 (https://geobaliza.com/products/baliza-v16-sos-flash-evo-conectada-certificada-dgt) features SOS Flash/INNOVA branding, a brief product description, Telefónica connectivity, downloadable data sheet (https://cdn.shopify.com/s/files/1/0918/8786/2086/files/FT_SOSFLASH_OK.pdf?v=1766569978) and user manual (https://cdn.shopify.com/s/files/1/0918/8786/2086/files/sosflash-evo-manual.pdf?v=1761057481). Device with certificate number LCOE 2025080734G1 (https://geobaliza.com/products/fase-light-v16-iot-conectada-certificada-dgt) features Fase Light branding, a brief product description, Telefónica connectivity, and downloadable data sheet (https://cdn.shopify.com/s/files/1/0918/8786/2086/files/FT_FASELIGHT_OK.pdf?v=1766569978) but no user manual. Device with certificate number IDIADA PC23100726 (https://geobaliza.com/products/baliza-ksix-v16-conectada-a-dgt-con-orange-con-funda) features KSIX branding, a brief product description, Orange connectivity, downloadable data sheet (https://cdn.shopify.com/s/files/1/0918/8786/2086/files/FT_KSIX_ok.pdf?v=1766234666) and user manual (https://cdn.shopify.com/s/files/1/0918/8786/2086/files/Manual-KSIX-T912.pdf?v=1766054107). Device with certificate number IDIADA PC24050316 (https://geobaliza.com/products/osram-v16-iot-conectada-certificada-dgt) features OSRAM branding, a brief product description, Telefónica connectivity, a downloadable data sheet (https://cdn.shopify.com/s/files/1/0918/8786/2086/files/FT_OSRAM_OK.pdf?v=1766569978) and no user manual. Device with certificate number IDIADA PC25120125 (https://geobaliza.com/products/baliza-aleu-iot-v16-conectada-certificada-dgt-y-solidaria) features ALEU branding, a brief product description, Telefónica connectivity, a downloadable data sheet (https://cdn.shopify.com/s/files/1/0918/8786/2086/files/Ficha-Tecnica-Baliza-v16-Aleu.pdf?v=1764690408) and no user manual. However, data sheet does not correspond to device with certificate number IDIADA PC25120125: it is device (also branded ALEU) with certificate number LCOE 2023080717G1. Product illustrations match LCOE 2023080717G1. brand_notes: Official website found for Pool Line: https://www.pool-line.com/es/. Site features several categories of car accessories in its catalogue. No purchase link/option appears available for any of the non-V16 products selected for inspection (10+). The V16 section features two distinct V16 items and ten kits/packs/accessories. Five of those kits/packs/accessories are missing a picture/illustration – upon selection, product profiles feature only a Pool Line logo in place of an illustration and a corporate reference number (e.g. https://www.pool-line.com/es/productos/accesorios/garaje/balizas-y-accesorios/balizas/pack-3-balizas-geolocalizada-the-boutiuque/) with no purchase option or any further information available. Illustrated items feature one or more pictures of kit/pack/accessory, a reference number, and no further information or purchase option available (e.g. https://www.pool-line.com/es/productos/accesorios/garaje/balizas-y-accesorios/balizas/pack-baliza-ventosa-adaptador-corriente-funda-pool-line/). Of the two V16 devices, one (https://www.pool-line.com/es/productos/accesorios/garaje/balizas-y-accesorios/balizas/baliza-v-16-geolocalizada-sos-geolux/) is pictured as a generic GELOUX device and is accompanied by two PDF documents available for download: a brochure (https://pool-line.com/resources/files/202200-03.pdf) featuring the certificate number IDIADA PC25030101 and Telefónica connectivity; and a user manual (https://pool-line.com/resources/files/202200-04.pdf) featuring no certificate number. Certificate number in brochure matches GELOUX-branded device approved by DGT. No purchase option available. The other V16 device (https://www.pool-line.com/es/productos/accesorios/garaje/balizas-y-accesorios/balizas/baliza-v-16-geolocalizada-pool-line/) is pictured with Pool Line branding and certificate number IDIADA PC25030099. Two PDFs are also available for download: a brochure (https://pool-line.com/resources/files/202100-11.pdf) featuring the certificate number IDIADA PC25060211 (does not match device profile pictures) and Telefónica connectivity; and user manual (https://pool-line.com/resources/files/202100-12.pdf) featuring certificate number IDIADA PC25060211 again and Telefónica connectivity. No purchase option available.

lab_notes: Official website found for LCOE: https://www.lcoe.es. LCOE (Laboratorio Central Oficial de Electrotecnia) is operated by FUNDACIÓN PARA EL FOMENTO DE LA INNOVACIÓN INDUSTRIAL (F2I2; CIF G80455231, Madrid). F2I2 frames LCOE as institutionally continuous since 1949 (creation of the original electrotechnical lab), with the current foundation structure established in 1992 after reconversion of the ETSII/UPM industrial labs (“Patronato de Laboratorios Industriales”). LCOE also sits in international conformity-testing infrastructure as an IECEE CB Testing Laboratory (CBTL) under AENOR responsibility (certificate TL083; acceptance documentation issued 2018, with IECEE acceptance history shown in later updates). In Spain’s ministerial designation landscape, LCOE appears in MINTUR’s Jun 2025 roster as ST0001 (designated “Servicio Técnico” in the EU vehicle/type-approval ecosystem), with F2I2’s UCA separately listed as ST0113 (certification/CoP-type functions).

The site footer displays two LCOE certificate badge numbers: LCOE 2025020090G1 and LCOE 2023010084G1 linked to the respective DGT certificate PDFs (https://ledone.eco/wp-content/uploads/2025/11/LCOE-2025020090G1.pdf, https://ledone.eco/wp-content/uploads/2026/01/LCOE-2023010084G1_Ext-1-2025070646G1-3.pdf – Certificate number on DGT whitelist and LEDOne badge (LCOE 2023010084G1) does not match certificate number on PDF document available for download on DGT or LEDOne sites (LCOE 2025070646G1)). lab_notes: Official website found for LCOE: https://www.lcoe.es. LCOE (Laboratorio Central Oficial de Electrotecnia) is operated by FUNDACIÓN PARA EL FOMENTO DE LA INNOVACIÓN INDUSTRIAL (F2I2; CIF G80455231, Madrid). F2I2 frames LCOE as institutionally continuous since 1949 (creation of the original electrotechnical lab), with the current foundation structure established in 1992 after reconversion of the ETSII/UPM industrial labs (“Patronato de Laboratorios Industriales”). LCOE also sits in international conformity-testing infrastructure as an IECEE CB Testing Laboratory (CBTL) under AENOR responsibility (certificate TL083; acceptance documentation issued 2018, with IECEE acceptance history shown in later updates). In Spain’s ministerial designation landscape, LCOE appears in MINTUR’s Jun 2025 roster as ST0001 (designated “Servicio Técnico” in the EU vehicle/type-approval ecosystem), with F2I2’s UCA separately listed as ST0113 (certification/CoP-type functions).

eu_DoC: EU Declaration of Conformity (manufacturer DoC). DoC holder/responsible manufacturer: INNOVA CELULAR S.L. (Spain; Rivas Vaciamadrid, Madrid). Product identified as 'Baliza V16 Geolocalizada EVO (SOS FLASH CONNECTED),' model BAL/4 (V16 emergency light with GNSS + NB-IoT; 'EVO' evolution of BAL/2). Declares conformity with RED 2014/53/EU and RoHS (2011/65/EU as amended by (EU) 2015/863) and lists harmonised standards for safety/EMC/radio identical to BAL/2. Scope not addressed in this DoC: issued May 2025, three months before EN 18031 enforcement, yet does not address RED Article 3(3)(d/e/f) cybersecurity/privacy/anti-fraud requirements made applicable for in-scope radio equipment from 01-Aug-2025 via Delegated Regulation (EU) 2022/30, and does not reference EN 18031-x; does not list EN 62311 / EN IEC 62311 for EMF human exposure (RED Art. 3.1(a) gap — same omission as BAL/2); does not cover WEEE producer obligations; battery regulatory compliance not set out in this DoC. Note: The DoC issuer (Innova Celular S.L.) differs from the fabricante listed on the DGT certificate (Yuyao Jiming Electronic Co., Ltd.). lab_activity: CNAE: 7490 Otras actividades profesionales, científicas y técnicas n.c.o.p.
lab_notes: Official website found for LCOE: https://www.lcoe.es. LCOE (Laboratorio Central Oficial de Electrotecnia) is operated by FUNDACIÓN PARA EL FOMENTO DE LA INNOVACIÓN INDUSTRIAL (F2I2; CIF G80455231, Madrid). F2I2 frames LCOE as institutionally continuous since 1949 (creation of the original electrotechnical lab), with the current foundation structure established in 1992 after reconversion of the ETSII/UPM industrial labs (“Patronato de Laboratorios Industriales”). LCOE also sits in international conformity-testing infrastructure as an IECEE CB Testing Laboratory (CBTL) under AENOR responsibility (certificate TL083; acceptance documentation issued 2018, with IECEE acceptance history shown in later updates). In Spain’s ministerial designation landscape, LCOE appears in MINTUR’s Jun 2025 roster as ST0001 (designated “Servicio Técnico” in the EU vehicle/type-approval ecosystem), with F2I2’s UCA separately listed as ST0113 (certification/CoP-type functions).
eu_DoC: EU Declaration of Conformity (manufacturer DoC). DoC holder/responsible manufacturer: KEPAR ELECTRONICA S.L. (Spain; Zaragoza). An updated Kepar DoC for product 'FlashLed SOS' (trademark: FLASHLED; 8×LED; 9 Vdc) was obtained via Turisport S.L. on 4 March 2026. That DoC's type reference ('FlashLed SOS') matches this certificate's model designation ('FlashLED SOS'). The obtained DoC declares conformity with RED 2014/53/EU, RoHS (2011/65/EU as amended), RED Article 3(3)(d/e/f) cybersecurity requirements (EN 18031-1:2025, EN 18031-2:2025), and EU Battery Regulation 2023/1542. A prior-version EU Declaration of Conformity was also located during collection (source: indexed search-engine result; hosted at media.ircongas.com). That DoC identifies product as 'Flashled SOS' and declares conformity with RED 2014/53/EU and RoHS but does not address RED Article 3(3)(d/e/f) cybersecurity requirements, does not reference EN 18031-x, and does not cover battery regulatory compliance under EU Regulation 2023/1542. Source: provided by Turisport S.L. via email on 4 March 2026 in response to a documentation request sent on 3 March 2026. Delivered as LCOE.zip attachment (4 MB) containing three brand-specific DoCs.

The flagship product page is “FlashLED SOS V16 IoT Connected Beacon” (https://flashled.es/products/baliza-flashled-v16-conectada-dgt) featuring marketing product description, Telefónica connectivity and functional purchase workflow, but no certificate number or product-specific documentation. A dedicated “Certifications and compliance” page links directly to supporting PDFs: (i) DGT/LCOE homologation certificate LCOE 2023010020G1 (https://cdn.shopify.com/s/files/1/0624/3804/7999/files/Certificado_HOMOLOGACION_DGT_FLASHLED_SOS_LCOE_2023010020G1_SF.pdf?v=1676281745 – fabricante (Kepar) is not legible in this PDF version); (ii) an EU Declaration of Conformity issued by Kepar Electrónica S.L. for an “Emergency flashlight V-16 with geolocation (GNSS) and NB IoT” (https://cdn.shopify.com/s/files/1/0624/3804/7999/files/EU_Declaration_of_Conformity._Flashled_SOS.pdf?v=1676281745); and (iii) a DGT 3.0 connection certificate annex (DGT/Ministerio del Interior headed) referencing connectivity tests for the V16 device to the DGT3.0 connected vehicle platform and listing hardware/software versions (https://cdn.shopify.com/s/files/1/0624/3804/7999/files/Anexo_Directriz_Certificado_DGT30_Turisport_154a3848-f054-4335-9735-3e23caf16e76.pdf?v=1676281745 – signed 17/1/23). A user manual has also been found claiming to apply to LCOE 2023010020G1, LCOE 2023100843G1, and LCOE 2023100795G1 (https://cdn.shopify.com/s/files/1/0624/3804/7999/files/MANUAL_INSTRUCCIONES_FLASHLED_SOS-ES.pdf?v=1771924040). Legal disclosure is also present: the site “Legal notice” and “Privacy policy” identify the website as owned by Kit Personalización Sport S.L. (NIF B61407623), registered office C/ Vic 99-103, 08160 Montmeló, Barcelona, with contact info@flashled.es and phone 93 572 30 21.

lab_notes: Official website found for LCOE: https://www.lcoe.es. LCOE (Laboratorio Central Oficial de Electrotecnia) is operated by FUNDACIÓN PARA EL FOMENTO DE LA INNOVACIÓN INDUSTRIAL (F2I2; CIF G80455231, Madrid). F2I2 frames LCOE as institutionally continuous since 1949 (creation of the original electrotechnical lab), with the current foundation structure established in 1992 after reconversion of the ETSII/UPM industrial labs (“Patronato de Laboratorios Industriales”). LCOE also sits in international conformity-testing infrastructure as an IECEE CB Testing Laboratory (CBTL) under AENOR responsibility (certificate TL083; acceptance documentation issued 2018, with IECEE acceptance history shown in later updates). In Spain’s ministerial designation landscape, LCOE appears in MINTUR’s Jun 2025 roster as ST0001 (designated “Servicio Técnico” in the EU vehicle/type-approval ecosystem), with F2I2’s UCA separately listed as ST0113 (certification/CoP-type functions).
eu_DoC: EU Declaration of Conformity (manufacturer DoC). DoC holder/responsible manufacturer: KEPAR ELECTRONICA S.L. (Spain; Zaragoza). An updated Kepar DoC for product 'FlashLed SOS' (trademark: FLASHLED; 8×LED; 9 Vdc) was obtained via Turisport S.L. on 4 March 2026. That DoC's type reference ('FlashLed SOS') matches this certificate's model designation ('FlashLED SOS'). The obtained DoC declares conformity with RED 2014/53/EU, RoHS (2011/65/EU as amended), RED Article 3(3)(d/e/f) cybersecurity requirements (EN 18031-1:2025, EN 18031-2:2025), and EU Battery Regulation 2023/1542. A prior-version EU Declaration of Conformity was also located during collection (source: indexed search-engine result; hosted at media.ircongas.com). That DoC identifies product as 'Flashled SOS' and declares conformity with RED 2014/53/EU and RoHS but does not address RED Article 3(3)(d/e/f) cybersecurity requirements, does not reference EN 18031-x, and does not cover battery regulatory compliance under EU Regulation 2023/1542. Source: provided by Turisport S.L. via email on 4 March 2026 in response to a documentation request sent on 3 March 2026. Delivered as LCOE.zip attachment (4 MB) containing three brand-specific DoCs.

Companies House shows LEDEL incorporated 16/03/2023 at 41 Devonshire Street, Ground Floor Office 1, London W1G 7AJ (a multi-tenant registered-office service address, with 2,200+ companies currently registered at the address per Companies House) and filing dormant accounts for YE 31/03/2024 and 31/03/2025 (£80,000 called-up share capital not paid). A long-form homologation dossier for IDIADA PC24030069 (DAEWOO / V16IoT) ties the platform to specific HW/SW identifiers and documents NB-IoT/“DGT 3.0” connectivity testing and Telefónica onboarding; it also includes a product-family declaration indicating the same hardware is reused across applicants with only firmware digits varying. Dossier maps RED evidence to safety/EMC/radio (3.1(a), 3.1(b), 3.2) but does not include documentation of the RED cybersecurity essential requirements (3(3)(d/e/f)) applicable from 01/08/2025.

lab_notes: IDIADA (Applus+ IDIADA) is a major automotive design/engineering/testing & homologation lab complex in Santa Oliva (Tarragona) and a high-throughput laboratory node in Spain’s V16 certification ecosystem. The operator is Applus (Applus Services, S.A.), which describes IDIADA Automotive Technology, S.A. as run under an exclusive concession since 1999 with an 80/20 ownership split (Applus+/Government of Catalonia). The 2024 reconcession was structured as a reversion/tender cycle: CNMV disclosures state Applus’s 80% stake was subject to reversion at contract end (Sept 2024) and the Generalitat tendered a new 25-year term; public reporting confirms €428M paid for the 80% block with Generalitat retaining 20%. Generalitat “Idiada 2” planning positions the site to expand into connected/autonomous vehicle testing and homologation, raising independence/market-structure relevance for connected-device regimes.

solicitante_notes: Official website found for AUTO ACCESSORY S.L.: https://www.premierauto-es.com/. The website consists mainly of static images and generic marketing text, with no legal notice, company registration details, CIF, ownership data, or manufacturer identification visible on the site. V16 devices appear exclusively in a single image gallery showing multiple commercial brand names (HRFLECT, GPSTAR, PROV16, ORFLECT) associated with visually similar hardware, with no individual product pages, no technical datasheets, no downloadable documentation, no explicit linkage between models and certification data, and no e-commerce or structured distribution channels. Certificate reference overlays appear as post-production graphics on product images and include: IDIADA PC25040136, IDIADA PC25060141, IDIADA PC25070047, IDIADA PC25060140, IDIADA PC25070049, IDIADA PC25070048, and IDIADA PC25070050 — all of which match PREMIER AUTO ACCESSORY-associated DGT certificate numbers. The site contains no reference to the remaining 24 DGT-certified and PREMIER AUTO ACCESSORY-associated devices: IDIADA PC25060138, IDIADA PC25060139, IDIADA PC25070250, IDIADA PC25090002, IDIADA PC25090003, IDIADA PC25090254, IDIADA PC25090269, IDIADA PC25090270, IDIADA PC25090271, IDIADA PC25090325, IDIADA PC25100113, IDIADA PC25100380, IDIADA PC25100381, IDIADA PC25110126, IDIADA PC25110127, IDIADA PC25110197, IDIADA PC25110240, IDIADA PC25110241, IDIADA PC25120132, IDIADA PC26010070, IDIADA PC26010127, IDIADA PC26010128, IDIADA PC26010129, IDIADA PC26020029, IDIADA PC25100115, or IDIADA PC26020120. The same hardware variant appears across multiple commercial brand names (HRFLECT, GPSTAR, PROV16, ORFLECT), each associated with a distinct DGT certificate number; model designations (V16-2, V16-3, V16-2S) appear to share identical physical designs with branding differences.

lab_notes: Official website found for LCOE: https://www.lcoe.es. LCOE (Laboratorio Central Oficial de Electrotecnia) is operated by FUNDACIÓN PARA EL FOMENTO DE LA INNOVACIÓN INDUSTRIAL (F2I2; CIF G80455231, Madrid). F2I2 frames LCOE as institutionally continuous since 1949 (creation of the original electrotechnical lab), with the current foundation structure established in 1992 after reconversion of the ETSII/UPM industrial labs (“Patronato de Laboratorios Industriales”). LCOE also sits in international conformity-testing infrastructure as an IECEE CB Testing Laboratory (CBTL) under AENOR responsibility (certificate TL083; acceptance documentation issued 2018, with IECEE acceptance history shown in later updates). In Spain’s ministerial designation landscape, LCOE appears in MINTUR’s Jun 2025 roster as ST0001 (designated “Servicio Técnico” in the EU vehicle/type-approval ecosystem), with F2I2’s UCA separately listed as ST0113 (certification/CoP-type functions).

The operator is Applus (Applus Services, S.A.), which describes IDIADA Automotive Technology, S.A. as run under an exclusive concession since 1999 with an 80/20 ownership split (Applus+/Government of Catalonia). The 2024 reconcession was structured as a reversion/tender cycle: CNMV disclosures state Applus’s 80% stake was subject to reversion at contract end (Sept 2024) and the Generalitat tendered a new 25-year term; public reporting confirms €428M paid for the 80% block with Generalitat retaining 20%. Generalitat “Idiada 2” planning positions the site to expand into connected/autonomous vehicle testing and homologation, raising independence/market-structure relevance for connected-device regimes.

brand_notes: Official website found for Pool Line: https://www.pool-line.com/es/. Site features several categories of car accessories in its catalogue. No purchase link/option appears available for any of the non-V16 products selected for inspection (10+). The V16 section features two distinct V16 items and ten kits/packs/accessories. Five of those kits/packs/accessories are missing a picture/illustration – upon selection, product profiles feature only a Pool Line logo in place of an illustration and a corporate reference number (e.g. https://www.pool-line.com/es/productos/accesorios/garaje/balizas-y-accesorios/balizas/pack-3-balizas-geolocalizada-the-boutiuque/) with no purchase option or any further information available. Illustrated items feature one or more pictures of kit/pack/accessory, a reference number, and no further information or purchase option available (e.g. https://www.pool-line.com/es/productos/accesorios/garaje/balizas-y-accesorios/balizas/pack-baliza-ventosa-adaptador-corriente-funda-pool-line/). Of the two V16 devices, one (https://www.pool-line.com/es/productos/accesorios/garaje/balizas-y-accesorios/balizas/baliza-v-16-geolocalizada-sos-geolux/) is pictured as a generic GELOUX device and is accompanied by two PDF documents available for download: a brochure (https://pool-line.com/resources/files/202200-03.pdf) featuring the certificate number IDIADA PC25030101 and Telefónica connectivity; and a user manual (https://pool-line.com/resources/files/202200-04.pdf) featuring no certificate number. Certificate number in brochure matches GELOUX-branded device approved by DGT. No purchase option available. The other V16 device (https://www.pool-line.com/es/productos/accesorios/garaje/balizas-y-accesorios/balizas/baliza-v-16-geolocalizada-pool-line/) is pictured with Pool Line branding and certificate number IDIADA PC25030099. Two PDFs are also available for download: a brochure (https://pool-line.com/resources/files/202100-11.pdf) featuring the certificate number IDIADA PC25060211 (does not match device profile pictures) and Telefónica connectivity; and user manual (https://pool-line.com/resources/files/202100-12.pdf) featuring certificate number IDIADA PC25060211 again and Telefónica connectivity. No purchase option available.

lab_notes: Official website found for LCOE: https://www.lcoe.es. LCOE (Laboratorio Central Oficial de Electrotecnia) is operated by FUNDACIÓN PARA EL FOMENTO DE LA INNOVACIÓN INDUSTRIAL (F2I2; CIF G80455231, Madrid). F2I2 frames LCOE as institutionally continuous since 1949 (creation of the original electrotechnical lab), with the current foundation structure established in 1992 after reconversion of the ETSII/UPM industrial labs (“Patronato de Laboratorios Industriales”). LCOE also sits in international conformity-testing infrastructure as an IECEE CB Testing Laboratory (CBTL) under AENOR responsibility (certificate TL083; acceptance documentation issued 2018, with IECEE acceptance history shown in later updates). In Spain’s ministerial designation landscape, LCOE appears in MINTUR’s Jun 2025 roster as ST0001 (designated “Servicio Técnico” in the EU vehicle/type-approval ecosystem), with F2I2’s UCA separately listed as ST0113 (certification/CoP-type functions).

eu_DoC: EU Declaration of Conformity (manufacturer DoC) — two versions collected. DoC holder/responsible manufacturer: OVERSUNENERGY, S.L. (Spain; Paterna, Valencia). Product identified as 'Baliza Conectada,' model T912 (V16 emergency beacon with NB-IoT + GNSS). Original DoC (15/05/2024): listed radio interfaces (Wi-Fi, Bluetooth, LTE) that the updated DoC (16/10/2025) states are not present in the commercialised product; declares conformity with RED 2014/53/EU, RoHS, WEEE, and EMC Directive; lists harmonised standards for safety/EMC/radio including EN 62311:2008 (a later version EN 62311:2020 has since been published) and EN 60950-1 (withdrawn and replaced by EN 62368-1, mandatory from 20 December 2020); no EN 18031 cybersecurity references. Updated DoC + Annex (16/10/2025): corrects product to NB-IoT (3GPP Rel. 13/14) and GNSS only; argues EN 18031 and RED Art. 3(3)(d/e/f) do not apply on the basis that the T912 is not 'internet-connected radio equipment,' citing closed private-APN architecture with no internet breakout, absence of Wi-Fi/BLE/LTE radios, and immutable connectivity configuration; cites CTI RoHS report (A2250174576103, PASS) and CCIC WEEE report (20240210R03118X-2, 92.22% reuse/recycling). Scope not addressed in this DoC: EN 18031 exemption in the updated DoC is based on a private-APN 'not internet-connected' argument; this project did not assess how that argument would be evaluated by market surveillance authorities. battery regulatory compliance (EU) 2023/1542 not set out in either version. Source: provided upon request by OVERSUNENERGY, S.L. via email.

lab_notes: Official website found for LCOE: https://www.lcoe.es. LCOE (Laboratorio Central Oficial de Electrotecnia) is operated by FUNDACIÓN PARA EL FOMENTO DE LA INNOVACIÓN INDUSTRIAL (F2I2; CIF G80455231, Madrid). F2I2 frames LCOE as institutionally continuous since 1949 (creation of the original electrotechnical lab), with the current foundation structure established in 1992 after reconversion of the ETSII/UPM industrial labs (“Patronato de Laboratorios Industriales”). LCOE also sits in international conformity-testing infrastructure as an IECEE CB Testing Laboratory (CBTL) under AENOR responsibility (certificate TL083; acceptance documentation issued 2018, with IECEE acceptance history shown in later updates). In Spain’s ministerial designation landscape, LCOE appears in MINTUR’s Jun 2025 roster as ST0001 (designated “Servicio Técnico” in the EU vehicle/type-approval ecosystem), with F2I2’s UCA separately listed as ST0113 (certification/CoP-type functions).

lab_notes: IDIADA (Applus+ IDIADA) is a major automotive design/engineering/testing & homologation lab complex in Santa Oliva (Tarragona) and a high-throughput laboratory node in Spain’s V16 certification ecosystem. The operator is Applus (Applus Services, S.A.), which describes IDIADA Automotive Technology, S.A. as run under an exclusive concession since 1999 with an 80/20 ownership split (Applus+/Government of Catalonia). The 2024 reconcession was structured as a reversion/tender cycle: CNMV disclosures state Applus’s 80% stake was subject to reversion at contract end (Sept 2024) and the Generalitat tendered a new 25-year term; public reporting confirms €428M paid for the 80% block with Generalitat retaining 20%. Generalitat “Idiada 2” planning positions the site to expand into connected/autonomous vehicle testing and homologation, raising independence/market-structure relevance for connected-device regimes.

The website consists mainly of static images and generic marketing text, with no legal notice, company registration details, CIF, ownership data, or manufacturer identification visible on the site. lab_notes: Official website found for LCOE: https://www.lcoe.es. LCOE (Laboratorio Central Oficial de Electrotecnia) is operated by FUNDACIÓN PARA EL FOMENTO DE LA INNOVACIÓN INDUSTRIAL (F2I2; CIF G80455231, Madrid). F2I2 frames LCOE as institutionally continuous since 1949 (creation of the original electrotechnical lab), with the current foundation structure established in 1992 after reconversion of the ETSII/UPM industrial labs (“Patronato de Laboratorios Industriales”). LCOE also sits in international conformity-testing infrastructure as an IECEE CB Testing Laboratory (CBTL) under AENOR responsibility (certificate TL083; acceptance documentation issued 2018, with IECEE acceptance history shown in later updates). In Spain’s ministerial designation landscape, LCOE appears in MINTUR’s Jun 2025 roster as ST0001 (designated “Servicio Técnico” in the EU vehicle/type-approval ecosystem), with F2I2’s UCA separately listed as ST0113 (certification/CoP-type functions).

solicitante_notes: Official website found for AUTO ACCESSORY S.L.: https://www.premierauto-es.com/. The website consists mainly of static images and generic marketing text, with no legal notice, company registration details, CIF, ownership data, or manufacturer identification visible on the site. V16 devices appear exclusively in a single image gallery showing multiple commercial brand names (HRFLECT, GPSTAR, PROV16, ORFLECT) associated with visually similar hardware, with no individual product pages, no technical datasheets, no downloadable documentation, no explicit linkage between models and certification data, and no e-commerce or structured distribution channels. The site contains no reference to the remaining 24 DGT-certified and PREMIER AUTO ACCESSORY-associated devices: IDIADA PC25060138, IDIADA PC25060139, IDIADA PC25070250, IDIADA PC25090002, IDIADA PC25090003, IDIADA PC25090254, IDIADA PC25090269, IDIADA PC25090270, IDIADA PC25090271, IDIADA PC25090325, IDIADA PC25100113, IDIADA PC25100380, IDIADA PC25100381, IDIADA PC25110126, IDIADA PC25110127, IDIADA PC25110197, IDIADA PC25110240, IDIADA PC25110241, IDIADA PC25120132, IDIADA PC26010070, IDIADA PC26010127, IDIADA PC26010128, IDIADA PC26010129, IDIADA PC26020029, IDIADA PC25100115, or IDIADA PC26020120. The same hardware variant appears across multiple commercial brand names (HRFLECT, GPSTAR, PROV16, ORFLECT), each associated with a distinct DGT certificate number; model designations (V16-2, V16-3, V16-2S) appear to share identical physical designs with branding differences.

The site strongly markets long-duration connectivity (“conectividad ilimitada incluida” / “13 años” messaging) and “conectado a DGT 3.0” positioning, with product listing/compare pages and direct purchase flow. Site provides an explicit product-to-certificate mapping of the eight different V16 devices on its own product page and purchase flow within site. Device with certificate number LCOE 2025100950G1 (https://geobaliza.com/products/baliza-geobaliza-iot-v16-conectada-y-certificada-dgt) features GEOBALIZA branding, a brief product description, Telefónica connectivity, downloadable data sheet (https://cdn.shopify.com/s/files/1/0918/8786/2086/files/Ficha-Tecnica-GEOBALIZA-IoT-v3.pdf?v=1770888766) and user manual (https://cdn.shopify.com/s/files/1/0918/8786/2086/files/Manual-de-uso-GEOBALIZA-IoT-V16.pdf?v=1765995003). Device with certificate number LCOE 2025020066G1 (https://geobaliza.com/products/baliza-v16-trophy-conectada-certificada-dgt) features TROPHY branding, a brief product description, Telefónica connectivity, downloadable data sheet (https://cdn.shopify.com/s/files/1/0918/8786/2086/files/FT_TROPHY_OK.pdf?v=1766569978) but no user manual. Device with certificate number LCOE 2024070742G1 (https://geobaliza.com/products/baliza-help-flash-iot-conectada-y-certificada-dgt) features Help Flash + branding, a brief product description, Telefónica connectivity, downloadable data sheet (https://cdn.shopify.com/s/files/1/0918/8786/2086/files/Ficha-Tecnica-Baliza-v16-Helpflash-IoT-plus-v2.pdf?v=1770292136) but no user manual. However, product description later refers to certificate number as LCOE 2022110790G1 and data sheet corresponds to LCOE 2025070664G1. Device with certificate number IDIADA PC25040020 (https://geobaliza.com/products/baliza-v16-sos-flash-evo-conectada-certificada-dgt) features SOS Flash/INNOVA branding, a brief product description, Telefónica connectivity, downloadable data sheet (https://cdn.shopify.com/s/files/1/0918/8786/2086/files/FT_SOSFLASH_OK.pdf?v=1766569978) and user manual (https://cdn.shopify.com/s/files/1/0918/8786/2086/files/sosflash-evo-manual.pdf?v=1761057481). Device with certificate number LCOE 2025080734G1 (https://geobaliza.com/products/fase-light-v16-iot-conectada-certificada-dgt) features Fase Light branding, a brief product description, Telefónica connectivity, and downloadable data sheet (https://cdn.shopify.com/s/files/1/0918/8786/2086/files/FT_FASELIGHT_OK.pdf?v=1766569978) but no user manual. Device with certificate number IDIADA PC23100726 (https://geobaliza.com/products/baliza-ksix-v16-conectada-a-dgt-con-orange-con-funda) features KSIX branding, a brief product description, Orange connectivity, downloadable data sheet (https://cdn.shopify.com/s/files/1/0918/8786/2086/files/FT_KSIX_ok.pdf?v=1766234666) and user manual (https://cdn.shopify.com/s/files/1/0918/8786/2086/files/Manual-KSIX-T912.pdf?v=1766054107). Site is a bare, largely non-interactive single-page landing with generic marketing claims (“Professional Connected V16 Beacon Manufacturer”) and no verifiable corporate disclosures (no legal notice, registered address, company number, VAT/CIF, management/ownership, or product-to-certificate mapping). No product catalogue, datasheets, manuals, DoCs, certificates, or purchase/distribution channels are provided on limburg-tech.com itself. Official website found for Challux: https://www.challux.com. IDIADA PC21020060 does not appear on the current or past DGT homologation lists reviewed during collection. V16 devices appear exclusively in a single image gallery showing multiple commercial brand names (HRFLECT, GPSTAR, PROV16, ORFLECT) associated with visually similar hardware, with no individual product pages, no technical datasheets, no downloadable documentation, no explicit linkage between models and certification data, and no e-commerce or structured distribution channels. brand_notes:
Official website found for SHADOWSOS (Shadow SOS): https://grupocorredoira.com/shadow-sos/. Shadow SOS is a sub-brand of Grupo Corredoira, a Lugo-based B2B distributor of car electronics and audio equipment (the site is professionals-only, requiring registration to access purchasing). The Aviso legal (https://grupocorredoira.com/aviso-legal/) identifies the site owner as Corredoira Car-Audio S.L. (CIF B-27153105), registered address Rúa Noriega Varela (Poeta) 4, 27004 Lugo, España. The site is not V16-focused: the company's core activity is the sale and distribution of electronic equipment and car audio systems nationally, and its product catalogue spans radar detectors (Shadow-Stealth), GPS trackers (Shadow-Tracker), multimedia systems (Shadow-Multimedia), Pioneer, Nakamichi, GT Alarm, Bergstrom air conditioning, Aragón tow bars, and other automotive accessories. Shadow SOS «Baliza V16» is one of approximately 19 brand/product lines listed under the MARCAS dropdown menu.
PRODUCT DOCUMENTATION: The Shadow SOS product page (https://grupocorredoira.com/shadow-sos/) features DGT homologation badge and IP66/IK08 ratings. The page lists three products: one V16 device ("Baliza de señalización de emergencia V16 • BALIZA01," 25€ +IVA; https://grupocorredoira.com/producto/baliza01/) and two accessories ("VENTOSAV16" suction cup mount, 4€ +IVA; "Funda protección Baliza V16 • FUNDAV16," 7.50€ +IVA). The BALIZA01 product profile features certificate number IDIADA PC25100117 visible on the device imagery (label reads "IDIADAPC25100117, CADUCA 31/12/2036, LOTE 25-00XX"), brief product description, and claims operativity with all three Spanish mobile operators (Orange, Vodafone, and Movistar). Product description also advertises a "SERVICIO DE AVISO A TERCEROS CON COSTE ADICIONAL DE 3 EUROS" — a companion app allowing users to designate up to 3 contacts for beacon activation alerts. An embedded YouTube video provides app usage instructions ("Instrucciones de uso de la Aplicación de la Baliza V16 de ShadowSOS"). Connection with Shadow-Tracker localization system is mentioned but noted as "en desarrollo" (in development). A user manual is available for download from the product profile ("Manual usuario BALIZA01"; source PDF: Manual-BALIZA01_final.pdf). The manual identifies the device as reference Baliza01, confirms NB-IoT connectivity, DGT 3.0 geolocation transmission (anonymous, every 100 seconds after activation), CE RED (2014/53/EU) and RoHS (2015/863/EU) certification, and prepaid connectivity until the date indicated on the tulipa. Manual back page lists both Grupo Corredoira addresses (Lugo central delegation and Madrid delegation at Resina 33 G, Nave 14, 28021 Madrid). No EU Declaration of Conformity, DGT certificate PDF, or fabricante identification is available on the site or in the manual. Purchase workflow is functional within the site.

lab_notes: Official website found for LCOE: https://www.lcoe.es. LCOE (Laboratorio Central Oficial de Electrotecnia) is operated by FUNDACIÓN PARA EL FOMENTO DE LA INNOVACIÓN INDUSTRIAL (F2I2; CIF G80455231, Madrid). F2I2 frames LCOE as institutionally continuous since 1949 (creation of the original electrotechnical lab), with the current foundation structure established in 1992 after reconversion of the ETSII/UPM industrial labs (“Patronato de Laboratorios Industriales”). LCOE also sits in international conformity-testing infrastructure as an IECEE CB Testing Laboratory (CBTL) under AENOR responsibility (certificate TL083; acceptance documentation issued 2018, with IECEE acceptance history shown in later updates). In Spain’s ministerial designation landscape, LCOE appears in MINTUR’s Jun 2025 roster as ST0001 (designated “Servicio Técnico” in the EU vehicle/type-approval ecosystem), with F2I2’s UCA separately listed as ST0113 (certification/CoP-type functions).

Companies House shows LEDEL incorporated 16/03/2023 at 41 Devonshire Street, Ground Floor Office 1, London W1G 7AJ (a multi-tenant registered-office service address, with 2,200+ companies currently registered at the address per Companies House) and filing dormant accounts for YE 31/03/2024 and 31/03/2025 (£80,000 called-up share capital not paid). A long-form homologation dossier for IDIADA PC24030069 (DAEWOO / V16IoT) ties the platform to specific HW/SW identifiers and documents NB-IoT/“DGT 3.0” connectivity testing and Telefónica onboarding; it also includes a product-family declaration indicating the same hardware is reused across applicants with only firmware digits varying. Dossier maps RED evidence to safety/EMC/radio (3.1(a), 3.1(b), 3.2) but does not include documentation of the RED cybersecurity essential requirements (3(3)(d/e/f)) applicable from 01/08/2025.

lab_notes: IDIADA (Applus+ IDIADA) is a major automotive design/engineering/testing & homologation lab complex in Santa Oliva (Tarragona) and a high-throughput laboratory node in Spain’s V16 certification ecosystem. The operator is Applus (Applus Services, S.A.), which describes IDIADA Automotive Technology, S.A. as run under an exclusive concession since 1999 with an 80/20 ownership split (Applus+/Government of Catalonia). The 2024 reconcession was structured as a reversion/tender cycle: CNMV disclosures state Applus’s 80% stake was subject to reversion at contract end (Sept 2024) and the Generalitat tendered a new 25-year term; public reporting confirms €428M paid for the 80% block with Generalitat retaining 20%. Generalitat “Idiada 2” planning positions the site to expand into connected/autonomous vehicle testing and homologation, raising independence/market-structure relevance for connected-device regimes.

lab_notes: Official website found for LCOE: https://www.lcoe.es. LCOE (Laboratorio Central Oficial de Electrotecnia) is operated by FUNDACIÓN PARA EL FOMENTO DE LA INNOVACIÓN INDUSTRIAL (F2I2; CIF G80455231, Madrid). F2I2 frames LCOE as institutionally continuous since 1949 (creation of the original electrotechnical lab), with the current foundation structure established in 1992 after reconversion of the ETSII/UPM industrial labs (“Patronato de Laboratorios Industriales”). LCOE also sits in international conformity-testing infrastructure as an IECEE CB Testing Laboratory (CBTL) under AENOR responsibility (certificate TL083; acceptance documentation issued 2018, with IECEE acceptance history shown in later updates). In Spain’s ministerial designation landscape, LCOE appears in MINTUR’s Jun 2025 roster as ST0001 (designated “Servicio Técnico” in the EU vehicle/type-approval ecosystem), with F2I2’s UCA separately listed as ST0113 (certification/CoP-type functions).

Site is a bare, largely non-interactive single-page landing with generic marketing claims (“Professional Connected V16 Beacon Manufacturer”) and no verifiable corporate disclosures (no legal notice, registered address, company number, VAT/CIF, management/ownership, or product-to-certificate mapping). The page explicitly describes Limburg as a “professional connected V16 beacon manufacturer in China” and as a “related company of Cixi Challux Technology Co.,Ltd.”; the only functional navigation observed is a footer link labelled “Official website” which redirects to challux.com (separately reviewed), a “Contact us” link that opens the local mail client, and “Privacy policy” / “Terms of service” links that loop back to the same landing page. No product catalogue, datasheets, manuals, DoCs, certificates, or purchase/distribution channels are provided on limburg-tech.com itself. Official website found for Challux: https://www.challux.com. Site features physical address: RM 1505,XIANGGE BUILDING,No 1277 DEVELOPMENT ROAD,CIXI CITY,ZHEJIANG PROV,CHINA; pictures of workshop facilities: https://www.challux.com/about-us/; and a products section featuring exclusively ten V16 devices: five featuring certificate number IDIADA PC23100037 and five IDIADA PC21020060. IDIADA PC21020060 does not appear on the current or past DGT homologation lists reviewed during collection. Per Companies House filings for LIMBURG TECHNOLOGY LIMITED: SIC 99999 (Dormant Company); dormant accounts (called-up share capital not paid £20,000; net assets £20,000); four registered-office changes (291 Brighton Rd CR2 6EQ → CH default address Jan–Mar 2025 → 53 Whateleys Dr CV8 2GY → Adamson House M20 2YY → 37 Croydon Rd BR3 4AB); two compulsory strike-off cycles (25/02/2025–19/03/2025; 02/09/2025–28/01/2026), both discontinued following registered-office correction; corporate secretaries of record Yunma Tianlong International Consulting Co., Limited (Co. No. 09801282; appointed 27/07/2022, resigned 13/06/2023) and J&C Business (UK) Co., Limited (Co. No. 08182589; appointed 13/06/2023, resigned 03/04/2024).

lab_notes: IDIADA (Applus+ IDIADA) is a major automotive design/engineering/testing & homologation lab complex in Santa Oliva (Tarragona) and a high-throughput laboratory node in Spain’s V16 certification ecosystem. The operator is Applus (Applus Services, S.A.), which describes IDIADA Automotive Technology, S.A. as run under an exclusive concession since 1999 with an 80/20 ownership split (Applus+/Government of Catalonia). The 2024 reconcession was structured as a reversion/tender cycle: CNMV disclosures state Applus’s 80% stake was subject to reversion at contract end (Sept 2024) and the Generalitat tendered a new 25-year term; public reporting confirms €428M paid for the 80% block with Generalitat retaining 20%. Generalitat “Idiada 2” planning positions the site to expand into connected/autonomous vehicle testing and homologation, raising independence/market-structure relevance for connected-device regimes.

solicitante_notes: Official website found for Finder V16 Solution Limited: https://www.finder-v16iot-beacon.es/. Site presents Finder as a connected V16 (IoT) beacon manufacturer and OEM/ODM supplier, claiming “V16 projects since 2018” and geolocated V16 focus since 2021, plus generic capability statements (PCBA/EMC/RF testing imagery; “advanced technology”, “quality assurance”, rapid turnaround claims such as “12H/48H/7D”). The only clearly product-specific content identified is for “Baliza V16 TWL043”, explicitly tied to certificate IDIADA PC25100314, featuring a brief product description but no user manual, technical data sheet, EU DoC, or any relevant documentation. A prominent CTA offers to “Descargar certificado PDF” (certificate download) for the DGT-whitelist certificate, but it only opens a contact request window. There is no purchase option/link available. The pages shown do not provide a clear legal notice with UK company number/VAT, directors/ownership, or a verifiable mapping of the website entity to the UK registrant; instead the footer/contact area identifies the operator as “Finder V16 Solution Co.,Ltd.” and lists (i) a UK “branch” address: 291 Brighton Road, South Croydon, UK, CR2 6EQ, (ii) a China factory address: Zona Industrial Xinyunhuan, Camino Simen, Yuyao, Zhejiang, China, and (iii) contact channels sales@finder-v16iot-beacon.com and a +86 phone/WhatsApp/WeChat number. Companies House: FINDER V16 SOLUTION LIMITED (UK; Co. No. 15621714) incorporated 06/04/2024; director/PSC is Xuan ZHANG (China-resident; ≥75% control). J&C BUSINESS (UK) CO., LIMITED was appointed as secretary (06/04/2024–12/04/2024). The registered office was moved to Companies House Default Address (Cardiff) on 17/12/2024, followed by a First Gazette compulsory strike-off notice on 28/01/2025, then strike-off action discontinued after RO corrected on 12/02/2025 (current RO: 53 Whateleys Drive, Kenilworth CV8 2GY). In the DGT V16 ecosystem Finder appears in one row in the dataset: IDIADA PC25100314 (model TWL043; trademark Tiger Auto / WarnLight GEO PRO), with manufacturer listed as Hangzhou Tiger Auto Industry Co.,Ltd (China). Finder currently appears on a single certificate.

lab_notes: Official website found for LCOE: https://www.lcoe.es. LCOE (Laboratorio Central Oficial de Electrotecnia) is operated by FUNDACIÓN PARA EL FOMENTO DE LA INNOVACIÓN INDUSTRIAL (F2I2; CIF G80455231, Madrid). F2I2 frames LCOE as institutionally continuous since 1949 (creation of the original electrotechnical lab), with the current foundation structure established in 1992 after reconversion of the ETSII/UPM industrial labs (“Patronato de Laboratorios Industriales”). LCOE also sits in international conformity-testing infrastructure as an IECEE CB Testing Laboratory (CBTL) under AENOR responsibility (certificate TL083; acceptance documentation issued 2018, with IECEE acceptance history shown in later updates). In Spain’s ministerial designation landscape, LCOE appears in MINTUR’s Jun 2025 roster as ST0001 (designated “Servicio Técnico” in the EU vehicle/type-approval ecosystem), with F2I2’s UCA separately listed as ST0113 (certification/CoP-type functions).

lab_notes: Official website found for LCOE: https://www.lcoe.es. LCOE (Laboratorio Central Oficial de Electrotecnia) is operated by FUNDACIÓN PARA EL FOMENTO DE LA INNOVACIÓN INDUSTRIAL (F2I2; CIF G80455231, Madrid). F2I2 frames LCOE as institutionally continuous since 1949 (creation of the original electrotechnical lab), with the current foundation structure established in 1992 after reconversion of the ETSII/UPM industrial labs (“Patronato de Laboratorios Industriales”). LCOE also sits in international conformity-testing infrastructure as an IECEE CB Testing Laboratory (CBTL) under AENOR responsibility (certificate TL083; acceptance documentation issued 2018, with IECEE acceptance history shown in later updates). In Spain’s ministerial designation landscape, LCOE appears in MINTUR’s Jun 2025 roster as ST0001 (designated “Servicio Técnico” in the EU vehicle/type-approval ecosystem), with F2I2’s UCA separately listed as ST0113 (certification/CoP-type functions).

lab_notes: IDIADA (Applus+ IDIADA) is a major automotive design/engineering/testing & homologation lab complex in Santa Oliva (Tarragona) and a high-throughput laboratory node in Spain’s V16 certification ecosystem. The operator is Applus (Applus Services, S.A.), which describes IDIADA Automotive Technology, S.A. as run under an exclusive concession since 1999 with an 80/20 ownership split (Applus+/Government of Catalonia). The 2024 reconcession was structured as a reversion/tender cycle: CNMV disclosures state Applus’s 80% stake was subject to reversion at contract end (Sept 2024) and the Generalitat tendered a new 25-year term; public reporting confirms €428M paid for the 80% block with Generalitat retaining 20%. Generalitat “Idiada 2” planning positions the site to expand into connected/autonomous vehicle testing and homologation, raising independence/market-structure relevance for connected-device regimes.
eu_DoC: EC Declaration of Conformity (manufacturer DoC). DoC holder/responsible manufacturer: DONGGUAN CHONG GUANG LIGHTING TECHNOLOGY CO. LTD. (China; Dongguan, Guangdong). Product identified as 'Baliza V-16,' model MOD100 PLUS. Declares conformity with RED 2014/53/EU, Regulation (EU) 2019/1020, and RoHS (2011/65/EU as amended by (EU) 2015/863) and lists harmonised standards for safety/EMC/radio including EN 18031-1:2024 and EN 18031-2:2024 (RED Art. 3(3)(d/e/f) cybersecurity). DoC states IDIADA Automotive Technology S.A. performed specific examination under Real Decreto 1030/2022 (amending RD 159/2021) and issued certificate No. PC25020294; however, PC25020294 corresponds on the DGT registry to a different model ('MOD 2026,' not 'MOD 100 PLUS'), and this row's own DGT certificate carries a different IDIADA number — the certificate cross-reference does not align. Additionally, the same PC25020294 is cited in the MOD2026 PLUS DoC, which covers a different model. Scope not addressed in this DoC: does not reference WEEE Directive (2012/19/EU); battery regulatory compliance not set out in this DoC; no EU authorised representative/responsible person identified despite citing Reg. 2019/1020. Source: provided upon request by DONGGUAN CHONG GUANG LIGHTING TECHNOLOGY CO. LTD. via email.

Product illustrations match LCOE 2023080717G1. lab_notes: Official website found for LCOE: https://www.lcoe.es. LCOE (Laboratorio Central Oficial de Electrotecnia) is operated by FUNDACIÓN PARA EL FOMENTO DE LA INNOVACIÓN INDUSTRIAL (F2I2; CIF G80455231, Madrid). F2I2 frames LCOE as institutionally continuous since 1949 (creation of the original electrotechnical lab), with the current foundation structure established in 1992 after reconversion of the ETSII/UPM industrial labs (“Patronato de Laboratorios Industriales”). LCOE also sits in international conformity-testing infrastructure as an IECEE CB Testing Laboratory (CBTL) under AENOR responsibility (certificate TL083; acceptance documentation issued 2018, with IECEE acceptance history shown in later updates). In Spain’s ministerial designation landscape, LCOE appears in MINTUR’s Jun 2025 roster as ST0001 (designated “Servicio Técnico” in the EU vehicle/type-approval ecosystem), with F2I2’s UCA separately listed as ST0113 (certification/CoP-type functions).
