solicitante_notes: Official website found for LIMBURG TECHNOLOGY LIMITED: http://www.limburg-tech.com/ (not https). Site is a bare, largely non-interactive single-page landing with generic marketing claims (“Professional Connected V16 Beacon Manufacturer”) and no verifiable corporate disclosures (no legal notice, registered address, company number, VAT/CIF, management/ownership, or product-to-certificate mapping). The page explicitly describes Limburg as a “professional connected V16 beacon manufacturer in China” and as a “related company of Cixi Challux Technology Co.,Ltd.”; the only functional navigation observed is a footer link labelled “Official website” which redirects to challux.com (separately reviewed), a “Contact us” link that opens the local mail client, and “Privacy policy” / “Terms of service” links that loop back to the same landing page. No product catalogue, datasheets, manuals, DoCs, certificates, or purchase/distribution channels are provided on limburg-tech.com itself. Official website found for Challux: https://www.challux.com. Site features physical address: RM 1505,XIANGGE BUILDING,No 1277 DEVELOPMENT ROAD,CIXI CITY,ZHEJIANG PROV,CHINA; pictures of workshop facilities: https://www.challux.com/about-us/; and a products section featuring exclusively ten V16 devices: five featuring certificate number IDIADA PC23100037 and five IDIADA PC21020060. IDIADA PC21020060 does not appear on the current or past DGT homologation lists reviewed during collection. Per Companies House filings for LIMBURG TECHNOLOGY LIMITED: SIC 99999 (Dormant Company); dormant accounts (called-up share capital not paid £20,000; net assets £20,000); four registered-office changes (291 Brighton Rd CR2 6EQ → CH default address Jan–Mar 2025 → 53 Whateleys Dr CV8 2GY → Adamson House M20 2YY → 37 Croydon Rd BR3 4AB); two compulsory strike-off cycles (25/02/2025–19/03/2025; 02/09/2025–28/01/2026), both discontinued following registered-office correction; corporate secretaries of record Yunma Tianlong International Consulting Co., Limited (Co. No. 09801282; appointed 27/07/2022, resigned 13/06/2023) and J&C Business (UK) Co., Limited (Co. No. 08182589; appointed 13/06/2023, resigned 03/04/2024).

Dossier maps RED evidence to safety/EMC/radio (3.1(a), 3.1(b), 3.2) but does not include documentation of the RED cybersecurity essential requirements (3(3)(d/e/f)) applicable from 01/08/2025.

The page explicitly describes Limburg as a “professional connected V16 beacon manufacturer in China” and as a “related company of Cixi Challux Technology Co.,Ltd.”; the only functional navigation observed is a footer link labelled “Official website” which redirects to challux.com (separately reviewed), a “Contact us” link that opens the local mail client, and “Privacy policy” / “Terms of service” links that loop back to the same landing page. No product catalogue, datasheets, manuals, DoCs, certificates, or purchase/distribution channels are provided on limburg-tech.com itself. Official website found for Challux: https://www.challux.com. Site features physical address: RM 1505,XIANGGE BUILDING,No 1277 DEVELOPMENT ROAD,CIXI CITY,ZHEJIANG PROV,CHINA; pictures of workshop facilities: https://www.challux.com/about-us/; and a products section featuring exclusively ten V16 devices: five featuring certificate number IDIADA PC23100037 and five IDIADA PC21020060. IDIADA PC21020060 does not appear on the current or past DGT homologation lists reviewed during collection. Per Companies House filings for LIMBURG TECHNOLOGY LIMITED: SIC 99999 (Dormant Company); dormant accounts (called-up share capital not paid £20,000; net assets £20,000); four registered-office changes (291 Brighton Rd CR2 6EQ → CH default address Jan–Mar 2025 → 53 Whateleys Dr CV8 2GY → Adamson House M20 2YY → 37 Croydon Rd BR3 4AB); two compulsory strike-off cycles (25/02/2025–19/03/2025; 02/09/2025–28/01/2026), both discontinued following registered-office correction; corporate secretaries of record Yunma Tianlong International Consulting Co., Limited (Co. No. 09801282; appointed 27/07/2022, resigned 13/06/2023) and J&C Business (UK) Co., Limited (Co. No. 08182589; appointed 13/06/2023, resigned 03/04/2024).

lab_notes: Official website found for LCOE: https://www.lcoe.es. LCOE (Laboratorio Central Oficial de Electrotecnia) is operated by FUNDACIÓN PARA EL FOMENTO DE LA INNOVACIÓN INDUSTRIAL (F2I2; CIF G80455231, Madrid). F2I2 frames LCOE as institutionally continuous since 1949 (creation of the original electrotechnical lab), with the current foundation structure established in 1992 after reconversion of the ETSII/UPM industrial labs (“Patronato de Laboratorios Industriales”). LCOE also sits in international conformity-testing infrastructure as an IECEE CB Testing Laboratory (CBTL) under AENOR responsibility (certificate TL083; acceptance documentation issued 2018, with IECEE acceptance history shown in later updates). In Spain’s ministerial designation landscape, LCOE appears in MINTUR’s Jun 2025 roster as ST0001 (designated “Servicio Técnico” in the EU vehicle/type-approval ecosystem), with F2I2’s UCA separately listed as ST0113 (certification/CoP-type functions).

The website consists mainly of static images and generic marketing text, with no legal notice, company registration details, CIF, ownership data, or manufacturer identification visible on the site. V16 devices appear exclusively in a single image gallery showing multiple commercial brand names (HRFLECT, GPSTAR, PROV16, ORFLECT) associated with visually similar hardware, with no individual product pages, no technical datasheets, no downloadable documentation, no explicit linkage between models and certification data, and no e-commerce or structured distribution channels. lab_notes: Official website found for LCOE: https://www.lcoe.es. LCOE (Laboratorio Central Oficial de Electrotecnia) is operated by FUNDACIÓN PARA EL FOMENTO DE LA INNOVACIÓN INDUSTRIAL (F2I2; CIF G80455231, Madrid). F2I2 frames LCOE as institutionally continuous since 1949 (creation of the original electrotechnical lab), with the current foundation structure established in 1992 after reconversion of the ETSII/UPM industrial labs (“Patronato de Laboratorios Industriales”). LCOE also sits in international conformity-testing infrastructure as an IECEE CB Testing Laboratory (CBTL) under AENOR responsibility (certificate TL083; acceptance documentation issued 2018, with IECEE acceptance history shown in later updates). In Spain’s ministerial designation landscape, LCOE appears in MINTUR’s Jun 2025 roster as ST0001 (designated “Servicio Técnico” in the EU vehicle/type-approval ecosystem), with F2I2’s UCA separately listed as ST0113 (certification/CoP-type functions).

lab_notes: Official website found for LCOE: https://www.lcoe.es. LCOE (Laboratorio Central Oficial de Electrotecnia) is operated by FUNDACIÓN PARA EL FOMENTO DE LA INNOVACIÓN INDUSTRIAL (F2I2; CIF G80455231, Madrid). F2I2 frames LCOE as institutionally continuous since 1949 (creation of the original electrotechnical lab), with the current foundation structure established in 1992 after reconversion of the ETSII/UPM industrial labs (“Patronato de Laboratorios Industriales”). LCOE also sits in international conformity-testing infrastructure as an IECEE CB Testing Laboratory (CBTL) under AENOR responsibility (certificate TL083; acceptance documentation issued 2018, with IECEE acceptance history shown in later updates). In Spain’s ministerial designation landscape, LCOE appears in MINTUR’s Jun 2025 roster as ST0001 (designated “Servicio Técnico” in the EU vehicle/type-approval ecosystem), with F2I2’s UCA separately listed as ST0113 (certification/CoP-type functions).
